title: 9:14 AM: The Alert Storm
setup: You just logged in. The overnight deployment is on fire—checkout is returning 503s and latency is spiking. The team is panicking in Slack.
output: ERROR [09:14:23] checkout-service: 503 | p99 latency: 4200ms | affected region: us-east-1

A.text: SSH into the server and trace the failing service logs to isolate the bad commit.
A.result: You think like a Backend Engineer or Site Reliability Engineer—calm under logs, obsessed with root cause.

B.text: Message stakeholders immediately and draft a clear user-facing status update about impact.
B.result: You think like a Product Manager or Technical Program Manager—communication and trust come first.

C.text: Pull metrics into a quick dashboard to check if the failure correlates with a traffic anomaly.
C.result: You think like a Data Engineer or Observability Analyst—patterns in data reveal the story.

title: 11:30 AM: The War Room
setup: The rollback stopped the bleeding, but the CTO demands a permanent fix before the board meeting. Corrupted records are scattered across the database.
prompt: With leadership waiting, you decide to...

A.text: Refactor the checkout microservice with stricter contracts and circuit breakers.
A.result: You lean toward Software Architecture or Senior Backend—building resilient systems is your language.

B.text: Draft a 30-60-90 day roadmap that balances engineering fixes with stakeholder updates.
B.result: You lean toward Engineering Management or Product—strategy and alignment drive you.

C.text: Design an automated data-validation pipeline with real-time anomaly detection.
C.result: You lean toward Data Engineering or ML Ops—clean, trusted data is your foundation.

title: 4:00 PM: The Board Demo
setup: Your plan is live. The CTO asks you to prove to the board that this failure mode is eliminated, using whatever you delivered.
output: Deployment v2.4.1 live | error rate: 0.01% | validated records: 1,204
prompt: Standing with the projector on, you...

A.text: Run a live load test in the terminal to demonstrate the circuit breaker holding under stress.
A.result: You are a DevOps or Performance Engineer—proof is in the running system.

B.text: Present a user-journey slide deck showing restored checkout flow and satisfaction scores.
B.result: You are a Product Manager or UX Lead—value is shown through human experience.

C.text: Display the real-time validation stream and explain the model's confidence intervals.
C.result: You are a Data Scientist or Analytics Lead—insight comes from rigorous measurement.
